column2: ALTER TABLE `aqorders` MODIFY `quantityreceived` smallint(6) default NULL;
column4: The quantityreceived column allows NULL but a received quantity should always be a number. NULL values cause SUM aggregations to silently skip rows and give wrong totals.
column5: Change the column to NOT NULL with default 0.
column6: ALTER TABLE `aqorders` MODIFY `quantityreceived` smallint(6) NOT NULL default 0;
column8: Quantity columns should always be NOT NULL with a default to avoid silent errors in aggregations.

column4: The closing backtick of the column identifier is missing and there is no terminating semicolon causing a syntax error that aborts all subsequent statements in the script.
column5: Add the missing closing backtick and semicolon.
column8: Column identifiers must be properly closed with backticks and every statement must end with a semicolon.

column2: CREATE TABLE `users` ( `isBanned` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT '0', `isCreatorBanned` int(11) NOT NULL );
column4: The isCreatorBanned column is NOT NULL but has no DEFAULT value causing INSERT failures when the column is not explicitly supplied.
column5: Add DEFAULT '0' to the isCreatorBanned column.
column6: CREATE TABLE `users` ( `isBanned` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT '0', `isCreatorBanned` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT '0' );
column8: Every NOT NULL column should have a DEFAULT value to avoid insert failures in strict SQL mode.

column2: ALTER TABLE user_plots ADD packet_rid integer NULL REFERENCES packets (packet_uid) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE; CREATE TABLE packets (packet_uid serial PRIMARY KEY);
column4: The ALTER TABLE adding a foreign key to packets was placed before the CREATE TABLE for packets causing the migration to fail because the referenced table does not exist yet.
column5: Move the ALTER TABLE to after the CREATE TABLE for the referenced table.
column6: CREATE TABLE packets (packet_uid serial PRIMARY KEY); ALTER TABLE user_plots ADD packet_rid integer NULL REFERENCES packets (packet_uid) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE;
column8: Always define referenced tables before adding foreign key constraints that reference them.

column2: CREATE TABLE allele_code ( allele_code_id int(10) unsigned NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, allele varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (allele_code_id), UNIQUE KEY allele_idx (allele(1000)) );
column4: The table was created without specifying a character set. Using utf8mb4 with a 1000-character prefix key exceeds the maximum allowed key length causing a creation error.
column5: Add DEFAULT CHARSET=latin1 to keep the index within the allowed limit.
column6: CREATE TABLE allele_code ( allele_code_id int(10) unsigned NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, allele varchar(100) DEFAULT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (allele_code_id), UNIQUE KEY allele_idx (allele(1000)) ) DEFAULT CHARSET=latin1;
column8: When using long prefix indexes on varchar columns ensure single-byte charset or reduce the prefix length.
